name: Mirror of Perseus
description: The Mirror of Perseus is not a single, explicitly defined artifact in Greek mythology but rather a conceptual tool. Perseus, tasked with slaying Medusa, a Gorgon whose gaze turned living beings to stone, needed a way to approach the monster without direct eye contact. Various accounts describe this tool as a highly polished shield, which functioned as a mirror, or simply a reflective surface. Athena, the goddess of wisdom and strategic warfare, is often credited with providing Perseus with this reflective shield or advising him on its use. By looking at Medusa's reflection instead of directly at her, Perseus was able to safely approach and decapitate her. The Mirror of Perseus, therefore, represents ingenuity, strategic thinking, and the ability to overcome seemingly insurmountable obstacles through cleverness and indirect methods. It symbolizes the importance of preparation and the use of intellect to defeat powerful adversaries. The reflective surface allowed Perseus to see Medusa’s true form without suffering the consequences of her petrifying gaze, highlighting the power of indirect observation and the limitations of direct confrontation. The mirror is a testament to the collaboration between gods and heroes, where divine assistance enables mortal triumph. It is a symbol of hope, showing that even the most terrifying monsters can be defeated with the right tools and strategy. The mirror's role extends beyond mere protection; it embodies the ability to see things from a different perspective, allowing one to overcome challenges that would otherwise be impossible. The act of using the mirror also speaks to the importance of self-preservation and the willingness to adapt to difficult circumstances.
subtitle: Reflection against the Gorgon's gaze.
origin: Gift from Athena or Hephaestus, or possibly a regular shield adapted for the purpose.
currentLocation: Lost to time, potentially returned to Athena or Hephaestus.

keyMyths.title: Perseus and Medusa
keyMyths.description: Perseus uses the mirrored shield to safely approach and decapitate Medusa, avoiding her petrifying gaze. The shield allowed him to see her reflection and guide his actions.

keyMyths.title: Athena's Guidance
keyMyths.description: Athena either gifts Perseus the shield or advises him on using a reflective surface to combat Medusa. Her wisdom is crucial to Perseus' success.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive source on Greek mythology, detailing the story of Perseus and Medusa.
sources.text: A comprehensive source on Greek mythology, detailing the story of Perseus and Medusa.
